text: This video demonstrates the technology and technical capacity of the Airsealer IFS made by SurgeEquest. This is an innovative access and suflation system that continually monitors and adjusts interoperative carbon dioxide flow rates to maintain a fixed volume pneumoperitoneum. It can immediately and automatically compensate for the pressure and volume variability caused by gas loss due to leakage or suctioning, thereby mitigating against diminution of pneumoperitoneum as a consequence.

The patient is positioned in lithotomy for the laparoscopic loop defunctioning sigmoid colostomy. The air seal trocar and tubing system are inserted subumbilically using the HESAM technique. A trephine incision is made at the preoperatively marked site in the left lower quadrant for stoma formation, with a wound protector placed and twisted to narrow the aperture.

The air seal system maintains a stable pneumoperitoneum despite an open circuit, with the pneumatic working space visible through an aperture up to approximately one centimeter squared. The wound protector retractor is adjusted to modify the size of the opening, ensuring adequate abdominal distension and working space.

Under direct laparoscopic visualization, a Babcock forceps is inserted through the stoma site to assess the sigmoid loop. The forceps then draws the mobile apex of the sigmoid loop through the abdominal wall for exteriorization, preparing it for maturation into a loop colostomy.

The operation was performed without complication and illustrates fully the technology's capacity and indicates this operative technique as a potential alternative to single port and trephine alone stoma formation.
